name: Oklahoma

examFacts.notes: Administered by Pearson VUE for the Oklahoma Real Estate Commission (OREC). The 80-question national portion and the 40-question Oklahoma portion are separate exams, scored separately, each needing a scaled 70 percent.

intro:
The Oklahoma real estate salesperson exam is a tougher test than its reputation suggests. The Oklahoma Real Estate Commission, the state agency most people call OREC, contracts with Pearson VUE to license new agents through two separate exams, an 80-question national portion with a two-and-a-half-hour limit and a 40-question Oklahoma state-law portion with a ninety-minute limit. Each portion is scored on its own and you need a scaled 70 percent to clear either one, so a strong national score does nothing to make up for a thin grasp of Oklahoma law. That split is exactly why Oklahoma-specific preparation carries as much weight as the national material.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. Oklahoma tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches, from the Oklahoma Broker Relationships Act that replaced traditional common-law agency with statutory broker relationships and the duties a broker owes every party to a transaction, to the powers OREC uses to investigate complaints and discipline licensees under the Oklahoma Real Estate License Code, the Residential Property Condition Disclosure Act with its separate disclosure and disclaimer statements on one to four family homes, the rules on psychologically impacted property, and the trust account and Residential Landlord and Tenant Act provisions a property manager has to follow. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Oklahoma-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free Oklahoma real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full Oklahoma bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real OREC practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the Oklahoma question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: The Oklahoma salesperson licensing exam has 120 scored questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Oklahoma state-law portion. Pearson VUE delivers them as two separate exams and mixes in unscored pretest questions, five on the national portion and ten on the state portion, that look just like the scored ones and do not count toward your result.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: You need a scaled 70 percent on each portion, scored separately. The national and Oklahoma portions are graded on their own, so passing one does not offset failing the other. If you clear one portion and miss the other, you only retake the portion you failed, as long as you finish both within the year your Commission approval to test stays valid.

faq.q: How much does the Oklahoma real estate exam cost?
faq.a: Pearson VUE charges $75 to sit the salesperson exam, and because Oklahoma discounts two exams booked on the same order as two-for-one, that single $75 covers both the national and the Oklahoma portions when you schedule them together. Retaking just the portion you failed runs the exam fee again. The fee is non-refundable and non-transferable. Passing the exam is separate from the $35 application fee you pay the Oklahoma Real Estate Commission to issue your license.

faq.q: How long is the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours of testing in total, though the two portions are separate exams. Pearson VUE allows two and a half hours for the 80-question national portion and an hour and a half for the 40-question Oklahoma portion. You can schedule them back to back on the same day or on different days, either at a Pearson VUE test center or through the OnVUE remote online proctored option, and you leave with an official pass or fail score report.

faq.q: Who administers the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: The Oklahoma Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered by Pearson VUE for the Oklahoma Real Estate Commission (OREC). You register and schedule your exam date through Pearson VUE.

faq.q: Is the Oklahoma real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It is a serious test, and the part that catches people off guard is the 40-question Oklahoma portion. The national half covers the same principles taught in every state, but the Oklahoma questions reward candidates who actually studied the Oklahoma Broker Relationships Act, the license code and OREC rules, and the state's disclosure requirements. Most people who struggle leaned too heavily on national material and underprepared on Oklahoma law. Honest reps on Oklahoma-specific practice questions are the difference between a first-time pass and a retake.

faq.q: What's on the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers property characteristics and land use, ownership and transfer of title, valuation and appraisal, real estate contracts and agency, the practice of real estate, property disclosures and environmental issues, financing and settlement, and real estate math, with contracts and agency carrying the most weight. The 40-question Oklahoma portion is built around four areas, the laws and rules affecting Oklahoma real estate practice and OREC's authority, the Oklahoma Broker Relationships Act, property management under the Residential Landlord and Tenant Act, and disclosures and hazards including the Residential Property Condition Disclosure Act. The laws-and-rules section alone is half the state portion, so it is worth extra attention.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish your required Oklahoma pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage move is grinding Oklahoma-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National principles you can pick up from any course, but the 40-question Oklahoma portion rewards repetition on the Broker Relationships Act, OREC's powers and the license code, the Residential Property Condition Disclosure Act, and trust account rules. The RealReady app gives you the full Oklahoma question bank plus the national bank, detailed explanations, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where you are weak.

The 20 Oklahoma practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Oklahoma bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Oklahoma real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Oklahoma question bank built for the Oklahoma real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Oklahoma law that trips people up, like the Oklahoma Broker Relationships Act, the Residential Property Condition Disclosure Act, and the rules on psychologically impacted property.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Oklahoma's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
